title: Canopy Growth Stock Surges On Impending Trump Administration Marijuana Reclassification
text: Canopy Growth shares surged dramatically following reports that President Trump intends to sign executive order reclassifying marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III. Stock gained 54% on Friday following regulatory development announcement. President Donald Trump signed executive order December 18, 2025 directing U.S. Attorney General to reclassify cannabis from Schedule I to Schedule III. Schedule III classification eliminates Section 280E tax penalties allowing companies to deduct ordinary business expenses, which could boost after-tax profits by 40% to 70%. Reclassification could add $50-70 million in annual tax savings for Canopy, enabling FDA-approved trials for vaporizers and oils. However, after signing, investors dumped shares rapidly with stock tumbling 12% despite impressive gains. Canopy Growth stated welcome of reclassification decision marking important step toward modernizing federal cannabis policy and advancing normalization of regulated market. Executive order represents significant policy shift but implementation remains ongoing through administrative processes.
